acceptance_targets: [chapter_02_prose_001] PASS if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:519, no more than two pivots use the exact 'She should have ... Instead ...' structure and the remaining pivots turn on visibly different syntax. FAIL if the revised chapter still repeats that same reversal more than twice.

[chapter_02_prose_002] PASS if, in the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:475, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:541, no more than two passages describe Eli's hold through room-space-weight metaphors and the other passages register the shift through distinct concrete consequences. FAIL if three or more of those beats still rely on the same spatial metaphor family.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:519, no more than two pivots use the exact 'She should have ... Instead ...' structure and the remaining pivots turn on visibly different syntax. FAIL if the revised chapter still repeats that same reversal more than twice.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same self-betrayal sentence engine four times: Lena knows the prudent move, then the prose flips on an 'Instead' beat. By the third repetition the syntax is announcing the turn before the turn arrives, which makes the surrender feel authored rather than freshly pressured.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite at least two of the pivots at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:519 so they do not use the same 'She should have ... Instead ...' reversal. Keep one if you want the pattern once, but make the others break through a different rhythm: direct action, bodily interruption, clipped shame, or a sharper object detail.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:475, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:541, no more than two passages describe Eli's hold through room-space-weight metaphors and the other passages register the shift through distinct concrete consequences. FAIL if three or more of those beats still rely on the same spatial metaphor family.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps reaching for the same room-space-weight metaphor to describe desire: his name in the room, the room reweighted, him taking up room, the apartment changing weight, the room staying shaped around him. Because the style bible already privileges spatial pressure, this many near-returns starts to read like token-list adherence rather than fresh perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the strongest one or two spatial-pressure images at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:475, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:541, but replace the others with different concrete effects of attachment: a work error, a changed body posture, a household object treated differently, or a social secrecy beat.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
